Title: Divergent multifunctional P450s-empowered biosynthesis of bioactive tripterifordin and cryptic atiserenoids in Aconitum implies convergent evolution

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Aconitum tissues and heterologous N. benthamiana production system
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: some atiserenoids showed allelopathic root-growth inhibition activity
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: functional TPS/P450 coexpression module in A. coreanum
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CfDXS; AtGGPPS; AcaCPS; AcoAS/ent-atiserene biosynthesis genes; AcoCYP725A1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS; LC-MS; large-scale purification and NMR; AlphaFold2 docking; site-directed mutagenesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: multifunctionally oxidizes ent-atiserene at multiple positions to generate diverse atiserenoids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoCYP93A1 is a multifunctional P450 acting on ent-atiserene. 2. It oxidizes the scaffold at three distinct sites to produce A8-A10. 3. Mutagenesis identified key residues, especially I404 and I241, that shift activity and product specificity. 4. Products contribute to the cryptic atiserenoid chemical space, some with allelopathic activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First report of CYP93A family members oxidizing labdane-related diterpene scaffolds; not a classical pathway enzyme category under requested scheme, thus placed as Common_Gene.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome; BioProject PRJNA1187874
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuous activity creates mixed product profiles unless combined with partner P450s.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong biocatalyst candidate for designer oxidation and diversification of ent-atiserene-derived diterpenoids

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: A. coreanum coexpression module with diterpene synthases
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AcoCYP716B1; AcaCYP93A1; AcoCYP72A1; ent-atiserene biosynthesis genes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; GC-MS; NMR of pathway products
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: multifunctionally oxidizes ent-atiserene at four sites and collaborates with partner P450s to increase specific product yields such as A13 or A15
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoCYP725A1 is one of the most multifunctional P450s discovered in the study. 2. It oxidizes ent-atiserene at multiple sites to generate products including A7, A13 and A15-related profiles. 3. In combination with AcoCYP716B1 or AcaCYP93A1 it boosts formation of dominant products.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A. coreanum-specific enzyme without obvious homologous pair reported here.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome; BioProject PRJNA1187874
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity may reduce single-product yield unless paired with additional P450s.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for combinatorial biosynthesis of novel atiserenoids and oxidation pattern engineering

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in node 4 with AcoAS2; tissue-associated expression in A. coreanum transcriptome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ionizes ent-copalyl diphosphate and cyclizes it to form 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor scaffold of tripterifordin and guan-fu diterpenoid A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: A. coreanum tuber for tripterifordin detection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive diterpenoids; tripterifordin has anti-HIV activity reported in the paper context
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CfDXS; AtGGPPS; AcaCPS; AcaCYP71A1; AcoCYP701A2; AcaCYP701A2-1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product 4 was newly identified from Aconitum spp. as an alternative precursor scaffold for downstream oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoTPS3 produces 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene in N. benthamiana. 2. This scaffold serves as the precursor for combinatorial biosynthesis of guan-fu diterpenoid A and tripterifordin. 3. Its discovery suggests an alternative precursor route in A. coreanum diterpenoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome; BioProject PRJNA1187874
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables production of 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene-derived diterpenoids including tripterifordin and guan-fu diterpenoid A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS; large-scale expression; NMR structural elucidation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful entry enzyme for synthetic biology production of 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene-derived bioactive diterpenoids

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with functional TPSs in A. carmichaelii transcriptome; fibrous-root associated diterpene module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: guan-fu diterpenoid A pathway intermediates and tripterifordin pathway intermediates via C20 oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes diterpene scaffolds at C20; on 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene generates C20-oxygenated products leading to guan-fu diterpenoid A and, with C19 oxidase, tripterifordin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms oxidation state required for target products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: tripterifordin detected in A. coreanum tuber; guan-fu diterpenoid A detected from combinatorial biosynthesis and plant tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive diterpenoids; tripterifordin anti-HIV context; guan-fu base-related diterpenoids antiarrhythmic context
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AcoTPS3; CfDXS; AtGGPPS; AcaCPS; AcoCYP701A2; AcaCYP701A2-1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a C20 oxidase independently evolved relative to Tripterygium TwCYP71BE271, implying convergent evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcaCYP71A1 is a validated C20 oxidase. 2. Coexpression with AcoTPS3-derived 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene produced intermediates and guan-fu diterpenoid A. 3. Combined with C19 oxidases it enabled tripterifordin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome; BioProject PRJNA1187837
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes C20 oxidation leading to guan-fu diterpenoid A and tripterifordin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; GC-MS; NMR; combinatorial biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable P450 module for engineering C20-oxidized diterpenoids including tripterifordin analogues

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: A. coreanum coexpression module associated with functional TPSs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C19 alcohol and aldehyde intermediates en route to tripterifordin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene at C19 to alcohol and aldehyde products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor oxidation state required for tripterifordin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: tripterifordin detected in A. coreanum tuber
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive diterpenoid with anti-HIV activity context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with AcaCYP701A2-1 for C19 oxidation in the reconstituted pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcoCYP701A2 oxidizes 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene at C19. 2. Together with AcaCYP71A1 it enabled the production of tripterifordin and related hemiacetal/dial intermediates. 3. Supports convergent tripterifordin biosynthesis in Aconitum.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome; BioProject PRJNA1187874
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables C19 oxidation required for tripterifordin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; GC-MS; comparison with authentic standard; NMR for related products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for synthetic pathway assembly toward tripterifordin and C19-oxidized kauranoids

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed diterpene module in A. carmichaelii
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C19 alcohol and aldehyde intermediates en route to tripterifordin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene at C19 to alcohol/aldehyde states
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor oxidation state required for tripterifordin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: tripterifordin detected in A. coreanum tuber
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive diterpenoid with anti-HIV activity context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A. carmichaelii homolog of C19 oxidase used interchangeably in pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcaCYP701A2-1 acts as a C19 oxidase on 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene. 2. In combination with AcaCYP71A1 it supports tripterifordin biosynthesis. 3. Indicates conserved C19 oxidation capacity across Aconitum species.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome; BioProject PRJNA1187837
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables C19 oxidation in the route to tripterifordin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful alternative C19 oxidase for engineered tripterifordin biosynthesis
